steps.title: Calculate both means
steps.content:
Data Set A: 9 values of 5.
$$\text{Mean}_A = \frac{45}{9} = 5$$
Data Set B: 9 values of 5, plus one value of 100.
$$\text{Mean}_B = \frac{9(5) + 100}{10} = \frac{145}{10} = 14.5$$
The means differ: 5 ≠ 14.5.

steps.title: Calculate both medians and identify the answer
steps.content:
Data Set A (9 values, all 5): The median is the 5th value when sorted.
$$\text{Median}_A = 5$$
Data Set B (10 values): Sorted as 5, 5, 5, 5, 5, 5, 5, 5, 5, 100. The median is the average of the 5th and 6th values.
$$\text{Median}_B = \frac{5 + 5}{2} = 5$$
The medians are identical. Therefore, only the means are different. That matches choice A — A is correct.
